key_dungeons.name: Burning Sanctum
key_dungeons.description: A narrow crevice leads into Burning Sanctum. The sound of dripping water echoes endlessly below.
key_dungeons.quest_objective: Search Burning Sanctum for the hidden Silver Key
key_dungeons.quest_hint: Rumors say it was sealed away behind deadly traps.

game_log: Doctor tinkers a Camping Supplies! (d20:7+3=10 vs DC8)
Jarraad: Be wary of going too deep and venturing far from town until you are ready

Rat Infestation: Hey therre, you look like a tough bunch. Tough enough to help with a little domestic problem. I am the owner of the General Shop, but I have a problem. You see, a while back I let a Goblin stay in my basement in exchange for... well, let's not get into that. But, since then he has let the place fall apart. Now it's full of Giant Rats and some of this vile kin. Can you deal with this? I promise, I can contribute to your adventures.
Rat Infestation: Wonderful! I'm counting on you. Good luck out there!
Quest 'Rat Infestation': Remove the Goblin - Complete!
Quest 'Rat Infestation': Kill the Rats, Part II - Complete!

Rat Infestation: Thank you, hero! Here is your reward: +100 XP, +100 Gold, +2 items (Camping Supplies, Arrows)

Old Dunmire: I was born in Larian, and so was my father, and his father before him. And none of us — not one — ever saw the sea smoke like it does now. Something wakes, traveler. Something old.
Old Dunmire: The shrine has stood a hundred years. It'll stand a hundred more, gods willing. But only if somebody puts the wyrm back to sleep — or ends it outright.

Meg of the Harborside: Aye, I've sailed as far as the Sun Citadel and back. Cold place, that. But beyond it — northeast, past the wyvern reef — there's an isle that smokes even in winter. No gull lands there. No sane sailor either.
Meg of the Harborside: If thou means to go, bring more than a boat. Bring fire, and a blade that answers to the sun, and a will to come back.

Sage Veylin: Sit, traveler. What I tell thee now leaves this room with thee alone. The unrest in these lands — the wolves, the orcs, the dead that will not lie — they are not the sickness, only its fever. The true blight is an ancient wyrm, older than Larian, older than any scroll I have read. It sleeps beneath a shattered keep on the far northeastern island, and as it stirs the land darkens. Arm thyself well. Take the Sun Sword if thou hast the arm for it. Descend through the broken halls, past the lava that feeds its breath, past the bones of those who tried before. End the wyrm. Cut out its heart and bring it to the shrine — only there may it be undone. Do this, and Asoloth yet has a morning.
Sage Veylin: Wonderful! I'm counting on you. Good luck out there!
Morvain the Grey: Thank you again, hero! Your deeds will be remembered.  [ENTER]
